title: tithymalus erythraeus
intro: Tithymalus erythraeus, also known as Euphorbia resinifera, is a succulent plant species native to the island of Madeira. It is known for its milky sap and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, thanks to its rich content of compounds like euphorbone, which has been shown to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties. The herbal preparations of Tithymalus erythraeus include resin extracts, which can be used topically or internally, and the plant's leaves and stems, which can be used to make teas and infusions for its medicinal benefits.
